verses.latin_vulgate: Muscæ morientes perdunt suavitatem unguenti.  Pretiosior est sapientia et gloria,  parva et ad tempus stultitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זְב֣וּבֵי מָ֔וֶת יַבְאִ֥ישׁ יַבִּ֖יעַ שֶׁ֣מֶן רוֹקֵ֑חַ יָקָ֛ר מֵחׇכְמָ֥ה מִכָּב֖וֹד סִכְל֥וּת מְעָֽט׃
verses.king_james: Dead flies cause the ointment of the apothecary to send forth a stinking savour: so doth a little folly him that is in reputation for wisdom and honour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cor sapientis in dextera ejus,  et cor stulti in sinistra illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֤ב חָכָם֙ לִֽימִינ֔וֹ וְלֵ֥ב כְּסִ֖יל לִשְׂמֹאלֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: A wise man’s heart is at his right hand; but a fool’s heart at his left.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et in via stultus ambulans,  cum ipse insipiens sit,  omnes stultos æstimat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגַם־בַּדֶּ֛רֶךְ (כשהסכל) [כְּשֶׁסָּכָ֥ל] הֹלֵ֖ךְ לִבּ֣וֹ חָסֵ֑ר וְאָמַ֥ר לַכֹּ֖ל סָכָ֥ל הֽוּא׃
verses.king_james: Yea also, when he that is a fool walketh by the way, his wisdom faileth him, and he saith to every one that he is a fool.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si spiritus potestatem habentis ascenderit super te,  locum tuum ne demiseris,  quia curatio faciet cessare peccata maxima.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־ר֤וּחַ הַמּוֹשֵׁל֙ תַּעֲלֶ֣ה עָלֶ֔יךָ מְקוֹמְךָ֖ אַל־תַּנַּ֑ח כִּ֣י מַרְפֵּ֔א יַנִּ֖יחַ חֲטָאִ֥ים גְּדוֹלִֽים׃
verses.king_james: If the spirit of the ruler rise up against thee, leave not thy place; for yielding pacifieth great offences.

verses.latin_vulgate: Est malum quod vidi sub sole,  quasi per errorem egrediens a facie principis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֵ֣שׁ רָעָ֔ה רָאִ֖יתִי תַּ֣חַת הַשָּׁ֑מֶשׁ כִּשְׁגָגָ֕ה שֶׁיֹּצָ֖א מִלִּפְנֵ֥י הַשַּׁלִּֽיט׃
verses.king_james: There is an evil which I have seen under the sun, as an error which proceedeth from the ruler:

verses.latin_vulgate: positum stultum in dignitate sublimi,  et divites sedere deorsum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִתַּ֣ן הַסֶּ֔כֶל בַּמְּרוֹמִ֖ים רַבִּ֑ים וַעֲשִׁירִ֖ים בַּשֵּׁ֥פֶל יֵשֵֽׁבוּ׃
verses.king_james: Folly is set in great dignity, and the rich sit in low place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidi servos in equis,  et principes ambulantes super terram quasi servos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָאִ֥יתִי עֲבָדִ֖ים עַל־סוּסִ֑ים וְשָׂרִ֛ים הֹלְכִ֥ים כַּעֲבָדִ֖ים עַל־הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: I have seen servants upon horses, and princes walking as servants upon the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui fodit foveam incidet in eam,  et qui dissipat sepem mordebit eum coluber.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֹפֵ֥ר גּוּמָּ֖ץ בּ֣וֹ יִפּ֑וֹל וּפֹרֵ֥ץ גָּדֵ֖ר יִשְּׁכֶ֥נּוּ נָחָֽשׁ׃
verses.king_james: He that diggeth a pit shall fall into it; and whoso breaketh an hedge, a serpent shall bite him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui transfert lapides affligetur in eis,  et qui scindit ligna vulnerabitur ab eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַסִּ֣יעַ אֲבָנִ֔ים יֵעָצֵ֖ב בָּהֶ֑ם בּוֹקֵ֥עַ עֵצִ֖ים יִסָּ֥כֶן בָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Whoso removeth stones shall be hurt therewith; and he that cleaveth wood shall be endangered thereby.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si retusum fuerit ferrum,  et hoc non ut prius, sed hebetatum fuerit,  multo labore exacuetur,  et post industriam sequetur sapientia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־קֵהָ֣ה הַבַּרְזֶ֗ל וְהוּא֙ לֹא־פָנִ֣ים קִלְקַ֔ל וַחֲיָלִ֖ים יְגַבֵּ֑ר וְיִתְר֥וֹן הַכְשֵׁ֖יר חׇכְמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: If the iron be blunt, and he do not whet the edge, then must he put to more strength: but wisdom is profitable to direct.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si mordeat serpens in silentio,  nihil eo minus habet qui occulte detrahit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יִשֹּׁ֥ךְ הַנָּחָ֖שׁ בְּלוֹא־לָ֑חַשׁ וְאֵ֣ין יִתְר֔וֹן לְבַ֖עַל הַלָּשֽׁוֹן׃
verses.king_james: Surely the serpent will bite without enchantment; and a babbler is no better.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verba oris sapientis gratia,  et labia insipientis præcipitabunt eum ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דִּבְרֵ֥י פִי־חָכָ֖ם חֵ֑ן וְשִׂפְת֥וֹת כְּסִ֖יל תְּבַלְּעֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: The words of a wise man’s mouth are gracious; but the lips of a fool will swallow up himself.

verses.latin_vulgate: initium verborum ejus stultitia,  et novissimum oris illius error pessimus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּחִלַּ֥ת דִּבְרֵי־פִ֖יהוּ סִכְל֑וּת וְאַחֲרִ֣ית פִּ֔יהוּ הוֹלֵל֖וּת רָעָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The beginning of the words of his mouth is foolishness: and the end of his talk is mischievous madness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stultus verba multiplicat.  Ignorat homo quid ante se fuerit ;  et quid post se futurum sit, quis ei poterit indicare ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַסָּכָ֖ל יַרְבֶּ֣ה דְבָרִ֑ים לֹא־יֵדַ֤ע הָאָדָם֙ מַה־שֶּׁיִּֽהְיֶ֔ה וַאֲשֶׁ֤ר יִֽהְיֶה֙ מֵֽאַחֲרָ֔יו מִ֖י יַגִּ֥יד לֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: A fool also is full of words: a man cannot tell what shall be; and what shall be after him, who can tell him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Labor stultorum affliget eos,  qui nesciunt in urbem pergere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲמַ֥ל הַכְּסִילִ֖ים תְּיַגְּעֶ֑נּוּ אֲשֶׁ֥ר לֹֽא־יָדַ֖ע לָלֶ֥כֶת אֶל־עִֽיר׃
verses.king_james: The labour of the foolish wearieth every one of them, because he knoweth not how to go to the city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ tibi, terra, cujus rex puer est,  et cujus principes mane comedunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִֽי־לָ֣ךְ אֶ֔רֶץ שֶׁמַּלְכֵּ֖ךְ נָ֑עַר וְשָׂרַ֖יִךְ בַּבֹּ֥קֶר יֹאכֵֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: Woe to thee, O land, when thy king is a child, and thy princes eat in the morning!

verses.latin_vulgate: Beata terra cujus rex nobilis est,  et cujus principes vescuntur in tempore suo,  ad reficiendum, et non ad luxuriam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַשְׁרֵ֣יךְ אֶ֔רֶץ שֶׁמַּלְכֵּ֖ךְ בֶּן־חוֹרִ֑ים וְשָׂרַ֙יִךְ֙ בָּעֵ֣ת יֹאכֵ֔לוּ בִּגְבוּרָ֖ה וְלֹ֥א בַשְּׁתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Blessed art thou, O land, when thy king is the son of nobles, and thy princes eat in due season, for strength, and not for drunkenness!

verses.latin_vulgate: In pigritiis humiliabitur contignatio,  et in infirmitate manuum perstillabit domus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּעֲצַלְתַּ֖יִם יִמַּ֣ךְ הַמְּקָרֶ֑ה וּבְשִׁפְל֥וּת יָדַ֖יִם יִדְלֹ֥ף הַבָּֽיִת׃
verses.king_james: By much slothfulness the building decayeth; and through idleness of the hands the house droppeth through.

verses.latin_vulgate: In risum faciunt panem et vinum  ut epulentur viventes ;  et pecuniæ obediunt omnia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִשְׂחוֹק֙ עֹשִׂ֣ים לֶ֔חֶם וְיַ֖יִן יְשַׂמַּ֣ח חַיִּ֑ים וְהַכֶּ֖סֶף יַעֲנֶ֥ה אֶת־הַכֹּֽל׃
verses.king_james: A feast is made for laughter, and wine maketh merry: but money answereth all things.

verses.latin_vulgate: In cogitatione tua regi ne detrahas,  et in secreto cubiculi tui ne maledixeris diviti :  quia et aves cæli portabunt vocem tuam,  et qui habet pennas annuntiabit sententiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֣ם בְּמַדָּֽעֲךָ֗ מֶ֚לֶךְ אַל־תְּקַלֵּ֔ל וּבְחַדְרֵי֙ מִשְׁכָּ֣בְךָ֔ אַל־תְּקַלֵּ֖ל עָשִׁ֑יר כִּ֣י ע֤וֹף הַשָּׁמַ֙יִם֙ יוֹלִ֣יךְ אֶת־הַקּ֔וֹל וּבַ֥עַל (הכנפים) [כְּנָפַ֖יִם] יַגֵּ֥יד דָּבָֽר׃
verses.king_james: Curse not the king, no not in thy thought; and curse not the rich in thy bedchamber: for a bird of the air shall carry the voice, and that which hath wings shall tell the matter.
